name: No-Cook Pizza Sauce
directions: [Skip this step if you're using fresh tomatoes] Mix and semi-puree all ingredients in a food processor for about 20 seconds. 'It may seem a little too runny, but trust me--it works!'-Tom Fitzmorris.
If you're using fresh tomatoes, just slice and place evenly on top of crust, brush them with the olive oil, and sprinkle the next 6 ingredients over the tomatoes.
Now pile on your favorite toppings and bake.  That's it!

name: Easy Pizza Sauce
directions: Saute onions and garlic in olive oil.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer uncovered until thickened, about 20 minutes.
Spread over pizza dough and add with your favorite toppings.

name: Easy Pizza Sauce- Homemade Gourmet
directions: Combine puree and suace mix in microwave-safe bowl.
Microwave on high for 3 minutes.
Let set for 5 minutes before using to top pizza.

name: Pizza Sauce.... Spoon Lickin' Good!
directions: In a medium saucepan, sauté garlic in oil until it just starts to turn golden.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer for 10 minutes over medium low heat.

name: Pizza Sauce
directions: Mix all in saucepan and simmer 20 minutes.  Top on favorite pizza recipe or dip for stromboli and mozzarella stick type appetizers.

name: Pizza Sauce
directions: Add all ingredients to saucepan, cover and bring to a boil. uncover, lower heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
this is also an excellent dipping sauce for bread sticks.

name: Creamy Pizza Sauce and Topping
directions: Place all ingredients in food processor and blend until smooth - make sure there are no tofu bits visible.
Spread the mixture on a 16" (approx) pizza crust of your choice.  Whole grain crust is healthiest.
Top with your favorite toppings such as mushrooms, black olives, chopped artichoke hearts, baby spinach, etc.
If you wish you can sprinkle a little parmesan or other grated cheese on top as well.
Bake like regular pizza - I use a 400 degree oven until hot in center, about 12 minute.
Notes:  Silken tofu is the kind that comes in the aseptic shelf-stable boxes.  As a variation, you can use chopped frozen spinach that is thawed and squeezed dry in place of basil.

name: Pizza Sauce
directions: In a medium sauce pan, saute garlic and onion in olive oil until tender.
Add remaining ingredients and cook on medium for an additional 10 minutes.
Use immediately or refrigerate for later.

name: Homemade Pizza Sauce
directions: Use a wire wisk to mix all the ingredients together and add the water slowly for a smoother sauce. for a thiner sauce use more water.

name: Homemade Pizza Sauce
directions: Use a wire wisk to mix all the ingredients together and add the water slowly for a smoother sauce. for a thiner sauce use more water.

name: Royal Pizza Sauce
directions: Make the sauce before you make the crust, so that the flavors have some time to get yummy.
Mix all ingredients in a big bowl; order doesn't matter. You can do without the Puree, and increase/decrease the amount of Sauce to conveniently match the cans you purchased.
This makes enough sauce for 2 big pizzas.  Refrigerate the remainder for up to 10 days before the texture gets lumpy.

name: Fast, Simple, Pizza Sauce
directions: Mix all ingredients together, and spread over the pizza base!

name: Favorite No-Cook Pizza Sauce
directions: Blend ingredients together in a blender until smooth.
You can use it immediately or store in the refrigerator for up to a week.

name: Pizza Sauce
directions: Warm the oil in a wide skillet with the garlic and a little black pepper.
Add the tomatoes and a pinch of salt and raise the heat.
Cook, stirring frequently until the juices are evaporated and the sauce that remains is thick enough to mound on a spoon with no surrounding watery liquid.
Taste and correct the seasonings.
